text:
Testing plays a vital role in modern software development. Unit tests check individual functions or components in isolation, ensuring each part behaves correctly. Integration tests go further, verifying that different components work together as expected.

A popular approach is test-driven development (TDD), where developers write tests before writing the actual code. This might seem backwards, but it helps clarify what the code needs to do.

By building a thorough suite of tests throughout the development process, teams gain confidence. With passing tests, developers can make changes and add features without fear, knowing that any mistakes will be quickly caught.
